easy.companyDescription: A specialized chip designer whose high-performance hardware serves enthusiasts and commercial computing buyers.
easy.macroContext: Cloud platforms and data-heavy workloads are increasing demand for specialized processing power.
easy.situation: Can a niche hardware category expand into larger commercial workloads without being overpriced as a cycle?
easy.longCase: New commercial workloads could expand demand beyond the original enthusiast market.
easy.shortCase: A cyclical chip market and volatile expectations make early growth easy to overprice.
setupHints: The company already holds a strong niche in performance-focused computing hardware.

medium.companyDescription: A semiconductor designer selling chips into multiple end markets.
medium.macroContext: Large computing operators are testing a wider mix of component suppliers.
medium.situation: Is broader demand a durable multi-year shift or a short product cycle?
medium.longCase: New buyer categories could expand the addressable market beyond the original niche.
medium.shortCase: Industry cycles and aggressive rivals can erase early gains quickly.

hard.macroContext: Infrastructure build-out is changing which hardware components get budget.
hard.situation: Will a niche supplier's demand broaden, or will cycles reassert themselves?
hard.longCase: If new workloads stick, component suppliers with the right products can re-rate higher.
hard.shortCase: Volatile expectations in chip markets make early growth easy to overpay for.

reveal.shortText: That was Nvidia. Demand for its specialized chips broadened well beyond the original enthusiast market, and the stock more than tripled.
reveal.funFact: Hardware once aimed mainly at enthusiasts became central to training modern machine-learning models.
whyItMoved: Commercial and data-center demand for specialized processors rose.
Investors re-rated the company's addressable market.
Performance leadership supported premium pricing for a period.

sources.label: Prototype sample seed
sources.notes: Placeholder sample content for Phase 3 pipeline; not production research.

review.reviewNotes: Medium/Hard rewritten after H023 Gate 2 identity failures (specialized high-performance processors + purpose-built data-center hardware triangulation). Intended peer set: Nvidia / AMD / Intel / Broadcom / Qualcomm / Micron-class semis. Easy unchanged; Medium/Hard Gate 2 pending re-judge.

direction.cue: New commercial workloads could expand demand beyond the original enthusiast niche

guesses.pointingFact: Jan 2015 semiconductor designer selling into multiple end markets as operators diversify suppliers

direction.cue: New buyer categories expand TAM, but chip cycles can erase early gains quickly

guesses.pointingFact: Jan 2015 semiconductor niche supplier hoping demand broadens with infrastructure build-out
Chip name where new workloads could re-rate suppliers if they stick
Infrastructure-linked semiconductor franchise with volatile growth expectations
Specialized semiconductor story easy to overpay for early cycle gains
Component supplier sensitive to cycles as hardware budgets shift

direction.cue: Durable multi-year demand versus reasserting chip cycles looks evenly matched
